items.title: Celtic reunite O'Neill with backroom team
items.description: Celtic announce that Shaun Maloney, Mark Fotheringham and Stephen McManus will continue to work under manager Martin O'Neill next season.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Haaland and Mbappé face off; Ecuador’s national holiday; Scotland still in limbo – live
items.description: ⚽ Latest news from day 15 | Haaland v Mbappé …

items.title: Mbappe v Haaland: Who is more important for their country?
items.description: Two of the greatest forwards in world football will go head-to-head at Boston Stadium on Friday, as France and Kylian Mbappe take on Norway and Erling Haaland.

items.title: Why Vrancken marks fresh start at data-driven Hearts
items.description: As Belgian head coach Wouter Vranckin takes charge at Hearts, it marks a strengthening of the club's data-driven vision, writes Thomas Duncan.

items.title: Hearts' Braga plays down transfer talk - gossip
items.description: Hearts striker says sole focus is on Champions League qualifying despite links to France as Celtic are set to end long negotiations with coaching staff...

items.title: ‘You better remember where you watching’: 30 years on from England’s loss to Germany at Euro 96
items.description: With those words on 26 June 1996, Des Lynam ended his coverage of England’s defeat by Germany, one of the most unforgettable nights in English tournament history. So we did …

items.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 19
items.description: Will you get today's World Cup player in as few attempts as possible?

items.title: What is SkyJo? The card game being played in the England camp
items.description: As England try to get their game right on the pitch, the games they play off the field are performing a key role in team bonding - as Morgan Rogers reveals to BBC Sport.

items.title: Turkey end World Cup on high with last-gasp win over USA
items.description: Turkey come out on top in the last minute of an end-to-end 3-2 win over a second string USA side, in their final Group D match at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.

items.title: USA suffer late defeat to Turkey but eye Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup knockout stage
items.description: The US men’s national team have already made history this summer. They scored the most goals they ever have in a single World Cup game, won two straight games in the tour…

items.title: Australia through and Paraguay close following draw between sides
items.description: Australia sealed their passage to the last 32 and Paraguay are almost certain to join them after they shared a drab draw in San Francisco which suited both sides.

items.title: From 42% to 5.26% - how Scotland's World Cup hopes are fading
items.description: Defeat against Brazil left Scotland in a precarious position, but little has gone their way since either.

items.title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
items.description: With the final round of group fixtures under way the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

items.title: Fifa unites the world – in anger at hydration breaks (AKA ad breaks) | Barney Ronay
items.description:
Fans, players and coaches have voiced their indignation at the way the game is massively altered by the four-quarter structure

With 22 minutes gone on Tuesday night at Bos…

items.title: Brobbey on target again as Netherlands beat Tunisia to win group
items.description: Brian Brobbey scores for the second game in a row as Netherlands defeat Tunisia 3-1 to top Group F, setting up a match against Morocco in the last 32 at the World Cup.

items.title: Japan and Sweden draw takes both into last 32
items.description: Daizen Maeda's smart finish to Japan's team goal is cancelled out by Anthony Elanga's long-range strike as Sweden fight back in a 1-1 draw that takes both sides through to the Worl…

items.title: Japan and Sweden both reach World Cup last 32 after Elanga’s strike seals draw
items.description: Thank goodness this is Dallas and not Gijon.…

items.title: Netherlands power to win over Tunisia and set up last-32 meeting with Morocco
items.description: The Oranje bus had better get a move on. A comfortable victory…

items.title: 'Oh my word!' - Elanga 'stunner' draws Sweden level
items.description: Anthony Elanga scores a brilliant curling effort from the edge of the area to draw Sweden level with Japan in their FIFA World Cup Group F match at Dallas Stadium.

items.title: Beccacece goes from brink to World Cup history with Ecuador
items.description: Sebastian Beccacece was on the verge of losing his job as Ecuador head coach as the clock ticked down against Germany - now he has a last-32 match to look forward to.
